projectDescription: A MATLAB tool for computing the L-curve criterion in Tikhonov regularization, including analytic curvature evaluation and automatic optimal regularization parameter selection.
keyFeatures: Analytic curvature computation of the log-L-curve (no finite differences)
Automatic optimal regularization parameter (lambda) detection via knee-finding
Multiple linear solver backends: SVD, backslash, inv, decomposition
Support for arbitrary regularization operators L (not just identity)
Matrix warm-up via SVD pre-computation for repeated evaluations
Returns L-curve coordinates, curvatures, and osculating circle centers
publicApiSurface: Lcurve(A) — warm-up: precomputes SVD of A for repeated use
Lcurve(A, b, L, LAMBDAs) — evaluates L-curve at given lambda values, returns XYKCs
Lcurve(A, b, L, LAMBDAs, METHOD) — same with explicit solver method
Lcurve(A, b, L) — finds optimal lambda (knee) automatically
Lcurve(A, b, L, []) — same as above with explicit empty lambda
[X_atKnee, K_atKnee] = Lcurve(A, b, L) — returns solution and curvature at knee
[Xs, Ks] = Lcurve(A, b, L, LAMBDAs) — returns solutions and curvatures for all lambdas
